subtasks.title: Minikube + Docker Setup
subtasks.prompt:
You are working inside the folder: c:\Users\piyushvi\Downloads\project\local-ai-orchestrator\

Task: Set up a local Kubernetes environment on Windows using Minikube with Docker as the driver.

1. Create the file scripts/setup-cluster.ps1 — a PowerShell script that:
   - Checks if minikube is installed, prints version if yes, prints install instructions if not
   - Starts minikube with: --driver=docker --cpus=6 --memory=16384 --disk-size=40g
   - Waits for minikube to be ready
   - Runs: kubectl create namespace ai-orchestrator --dry-run=client -o yaml | kubectl apply -f -
   - Prints the current minikube status and kubectl cluster-info
   - Enables addons: metrics-server, ingress

2. Create the file scripts/teardown-cluster.ps1 — a PowerShell script that:
   - Deletes the ai-orchestrator namespace
   - Stops minikube

3. Create the file docs/phase1-setup.md — a brief explanation of:
   - What Minikube is and why we use it for local K8s
   - What the ai-orchestrator namespace does
   - How to verify the cluster is working (kubectl get nodes, kubectl get pods -n ai-orchestrator)

Do not run any commands. Only create the files.

subtasks.title: PostgreSQL Sharded Deployment
subtasks.prompt:
You are working inside the folder: c:\Users\piyushvi\Downloads\project\local-ai-orchestrator\

Task: Create Kubernetes manifests for two PostgreSQL 16 shards in k8s/infra/postgres/

1. Create k8s/infra/postgres/postgres-shard-a.yaml — contains:
   - Namespace: ai-orchestrator
   - ConfigMap named postgres-shard-a-config with POSTGRES_DB=orchestrator, POSTGRES_USER=admin, POSTGRES_PASSWORD=admin123
   - PersistentVolumeClaim named postgres-shard-a-pvc with 1Gi storage
   - Deployment named postgres-shard-a with 1 replica using image postgres:16, mounting the PVC, using the ConfigMap as env vars, with a readiness probe on port 5432
   - ClusterIP Service named postgres-shard-a-svc exposing port 5432

2. Create k8s/infra/postgres/postgres-shard-b.yaml — identical structure, replacing all 'shard-a' with 'shard-b'

3. Create k8s/infra/postgres/init-jobs-table.sql — the SQL script that will be run to create the jobs table:
   CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS jobs (
     id UUID PRIMARY KEY,
     description TEXT NOT NULL,
     priority INTEGER DEFAULT 5,
     status VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'PENDING',
     shard_key INTEGER,
     submitted_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW(),
     updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW()
   );

4. Create docs/phase1-postgres-sharding.md explaining:
   - What database sharding is
   - Why we use two separate PostgreSQL pods instead of one
   - How hash(job_id) % 2 determines which shard a job goes to (shard-a for 0, shard-b for 1)
   - How to verify after applying: kubectl get pods -n ai-orchestrator, kubectl exec into a pod and run psql

Do not run any commands. Only create the files.

subtasks.title: Redis Deployment
subtasks.prompt:
You are working inside the folder: c:\Users\piyushvi\Downloads\project\local-ai-orchestrator\

Task: Create Kubernetes manifest for Redis 7 in k8s/infra/redis/

1. Create k8s/infra/redis/redis.yaml — contains:
   - Namespace: ai-orchestrator
   - ConfigMap named redis-config with a redis.conf that sets: maxmemory 256mb, maxmemory-policy allkeys-lru, save disabled
   - Deployment named redis with 1 replica using image redis:7-alpine, mounting the config as a volume at /usr/local/etc/redis/redis.conf, with command: redis-server /usr/local/etc/redis/redis.conf
   - Liveness probe: exec redis-cli ping every 10s
   - ClusterIP Service named redis-svc exposing port 6379

2. Create docs/phase1-redis.md explaining:
   - What Redis is used for in this project (job status caching, rate limiting)
   - The maxmemory-policy choice and why allkeys-lru fits our use case
   - How to test after applying: kubectl exec -it <redis-pod> -n ai-orchestrator -- redis-cli ping
   - How to inspect cached keys: redis-cli keys '*'

Do not run any commands. Only create the files.

subtasks.title: Kafka Deployment KRaft mode
subtasks.prompt:
You are working inside the folder: c:\Users\piyushvi\Downloads\project\local-ai-orchestrator\

Task: Create Kubernetes manifest for Apache Kafka 3.7 in KRaft mode in k8s/infra/kafka/

1. Create k8s/infra/kafka/kafka.yaml — contains:
   - Namespace: ai-orchestrator
   - A single-node Kafka StatefulSet named kafka using image bitnami/kafka:3.7, configured for KRaft mode (no Zookeeper) with these env vars:
     KAFKA_CFG_NODE_ID=1
     KAFKA_CFG_PROCESS_ROLES=broker,controller
     KAFKA_CFG_CONTROLLER_QUORUM_VOTERS=1@kafka-0.kafka-headless.ai-orchestrator.svc.cluster.local:9093
     KAFKA_CFG_LISTENERS=PLAINTEXT://:9092,CONTROLLER://:9093
     KAFKA_CFG_ADVERTISED_LISTENERS=PLAINTEXT://kafka-svc:9092
     KAFKA_CFG_LISTENER_SECURITY_PROTOCOL_MAP=CONTROLLER:PLAINTEXT,PLAINTEXT:PLAINTEXT
     KAFKA_CFG_CONTROLLER_LISTENER_NAMES=CONTROLLER
   - PersistentVolumeClaim of 2Gi
   - Headless Service named kafka-headless for StatefulSet DNS
   - ClusterIP Service named kafka-svc exposing port 9092

2. Create k8s/infra/kafka/create-topics.yaml — a Kubernetes Job that runs after Kafka starts and creates topics: job-submitted, job-routed, job-completed (each with 3 partitions, replication-factor 1) using kafka-topics.sh

3. Create docs/phase1-kafka.md explaining:
   - What KRaft mode is (no Zookeeper) and why it simplifies local setup
   - The three topics and what each carries in our workflow
   - How to test: kubectl exec into kafka pod and run kafka-console-producer / kafka-console-consumer

Do not run any commands. Only create the files.

subtasks.title: Qdrant Vector DB Deployment

   - How similarity search works (cosine distance)

subtasks.title: Workflow Controller Job Submission API
subtasks.prompt:
You are working inside the folder: c:\Users\piyushvi\Downloads\project\local-ai-orchestrator\orchestrator-parent\

Task: Implement the Job Submission REST API in the workflow-controller module.

1. Create workflow-controller/src/main/java/com/orchestrator/controller/model/Job.java:
   - A JPA @Entity with table name 'jobs'
   - Fields: UUID id (@Id), String description, int priority, String status (default 'PENDING'), int shardKey, Instant submittedAt, Instant updatedAt
   - @PrePersist sets submittedAt and updatedAt to Instant.now(), status to PENDING
   - @PreUpdate sets updatedAt to Instant.now()
   - Use Lombok @Getter @Setter @NoArgsConstructor @AllArgsConstructor @Builder

2. Create workflow-controller/src/main/java/com/orchestrator/controller/repository/JobRepository.java:
   - A Spring Data JPA interface extending JpaRepository<Job, UUID>
   - Add method: List<Job> findByStatus(String status)

3. Create workflow-controller/src/main/java/com/orchestrator/controller/service/JobService.java:
   - @Service class
   - Inject JobRepository
   - Method: JobResponse submitJob(JobRequest request)
     a. Generate UUID id = UUID.randomUUID()
     b. Compute shardKey = Math.abs(id.hashCode()) % 2
     c. Build and save Job entity with status PENDING
     d. Return new JobResponse(job.getId(), job.getDescription(), job.getPriority(), job.getStatus(), shardKey == 0 ? 'shard-a' : 'shard-b', job.getSubmittedAt())
   - Method: JobResponse getJob(UUID id)
     a. Find job by id, throw ResponseStatusException(NOT_FOUND) if missing
     b. Return JobResponse
   - Method: List<JobResponse> getAllJobs()
     a. Return all jobs mapped to JobResponse

4. Create workflow-controller/src/main/java/com/orchestrator/controller/web/JobController.java:
   - @RestController @RequestMapping('/api/v1/jobs') @Validated
   - Inject JobService
   - POST / -> accepts @Valid @RequestBody JobRequest, returns ResponseEntity<JobResponse> with 201 Created
   - GET /{id} -> returns ResponseEntity<JobResponse>
   - GET / -> returns ResponseEntity<List<JobResponse>>
   - Add @CrossOrigin for localhost:3000 only (not *)

5. Create workflow-controller/src/main/java/com/orchestrator/controller/config/JpaConfig.java:
   - @Configuration @EnableJpaRepositories
   - Define a single DataSource @Bean reading from environment variables:
     DB_URL (default: jdbc:postgresql://postgres-shard-a-svc:5432/orchestrator)
     DB_USER (default: admin)
     DB_PASSWORD (default: admin123)
   - Configure Hibernate ddl-auto to validate (not create -- we manage schema via SQL)

6. Update workflow-controller/src/main/resources/application.yml:
   - Replace datasource placeholder with proper defaults using environment variables
   - Add spring.jpa.hibernate.ddl-auto: validate
   - Add spring.jpa.database-platform: org.hibernate.dialect.PostgreSQLDialect

Do not run any commands. Only create or modify files.

subtasks.title: Database Sharding Configuration
subtasks.prompt:
You are working inside the folder: c:\Users\piyushvi\Downloads\project\local-ai-orchestrator\orchestrator-parent\

Task: Implement database sharding in the workflow-controller module using Spring's AbstractRoutingDataSource so each job is routed to postgres-shard-a or postgres-shard-b based on hash(job_id) % 2.

1. Create workflow-controller/src/main/java/com/orchestrator/controller/config/ShardContextHolder.java:
   - Uses a static ThreadLocal<String> to hold the current shard key per thread
   - Static method setShard(String shard) -- sets value on ThreadLocal
   - Static method getShard() -- returns current value
   - Static method clear() -- removes value to prevent memory leaks

2. Create workflow-controller/src/main/java/com/orchestrator/controller/config/ShardRoutingDataSource.java:
   - Extends AbstractRoutingDataSource
   - Overrides determineCurrentLookupKey() -- returns ShardContextHolder.getShard()

3. Replace workflow-controller/src/main/java/com/orchestrator/controller/config/JpaConfig.java entirely:
   - @Configuration @EnableJpaRepositories @EnableTransactionManagement
   - Define dataSourceShardA() @Bean: HikariDataSource reading from env vars DB_SHARD_A_URL (default: jdbc:postgresql://postgres-shard-a-svc:5432/orchestrator), DB_USER (default: admin), DB_PASSWORD (default: admin123)
   - Define dataSourceShardB() @Bean: HikariDataSource reading from env vars DB_SHARD_B_URL (default: jdbc:postgresql://postgres-shard-b-svc:5432/orchestrator), DB_USER, DB_PASSWORD
   - Define routingDataSource() @Bean @Primary: ShardRoutingDataSource with targetDataSources map {shard-a: dataSourceShardA, shard-b: dataSourceShardB}, defaultTargetDataSource: dataSourceShardA
   - Define entityManagerFactory() @Bean @Primary: LocalContainerEntityManagerFactoryBean using routingDataSource, scan packages com.orchestrator.controller.model, HibernateJpaVendorAdapter with PostgreSQL dialect, ddl-auto: validate
   - Define transactionManager() @Bean @Primary: JpaTransactionManager using entityManagerFactory

4. Update workflow-controller/src/main/java/com/orchestrator/controller/service/JobService.java:
   - In submitJob(): before jobRepository.save(), call ShardContextHolder.setShard(shardKey == 0 ? shard-a : shard-b), wrap in try/finally, call ShardContextHolder.clear() in finally
   - In getJob(UUID id): compute shardKey = Math.abs(id.hashCode()) % 2, call ShardContextHolder.setShard(), wrap repository call in try/finally with ShardContextHolder.clear() in finally
   - In getAllJobs(): call both shards -- setShard(shard-a), fetch all, clear; setShard(shard-b), fetch all, clear; merge both lists, map to JobResponse, return

5. Update workflow-controller/src/main/resources/application.yml:
   - Remove single spring.datasource block
   - Add comments showing DB_SHARD_A_URL and DB_SHARD_B_URL are read from env vars
   - Keep redis and kafka config unchanged
   - Add spring.jpa.hibernate.ddl-auto: validate
   - Add spring.jpa.database-platform: org.hibernate.dialect.PostgreSQLDialect

Do not run any commands. Only create or modify files.

subtasks.title: Redis Caching Layer
subtasks.prompt:
You are working inside the folder: c:\Users\piyushvi\Downloads\project\local-ai-orchestrator\orchestrator-parent\

Task: Implement the Redis caching layer in the workflow-controller module using a cache-aside pattern so job status lookups hit Redis first and fall back to PostgreSQL only on a miss.

1. Create workflow-controller/src/main/java/com/orchestrator/controller/config/CacheConfig.java:
   - @Configuration class
   - Define a RedisTemplate<String, String> @Bean:
     - Set connection factory from the injected RedisConnectionFactory
     - Use StringRedisSerializer for both key and value serializers
     - This keeps cached values as plain JSON strings for simplicity
   - Define a @Bean ValueOperations<String, String> that returns redisTemplate.opsForValue()

2. Update workflow-controller/src/main/java/com/orchestrator/controller/service/JobService.java:
   - Inject RedisTemplate<String, String> redisTemplate
   - In submitJob(JobRequest request):
     a. After saving the Job to PostgreSQL, write to Redis:
        redisTemplate.opsForValue().set(JobConstants.CACHE_STATUS_PREFIX + job.getId(), job.getStatus(), 5, TimeUnit.MINUTES)
     b. Return the JobResponse as before
   - In getJob(UUID id):
     a. Before querying PostgreSQL, check Redis:
        String cached = redisTemplate.opsForValue().get(JobConstants.CACHE_STATUS_PREFIX + id)
     b. If cached != null, construct a JobResponse from the cached status (fetch the full Job from correct shard for other fields, but use cached status value)
     c. If cache miss: proceed with shard routing + PostgreSQL query as before, then write result back to Redis:
        redisTemplate.opsForValue().set(JobConstants.CACHE_STATUS_PREFIX + id, job.getStatus(), 5, TimeUnit.MINUTES)
   - Add a new method: void updateJobStatus(UUID id, String newStatus)
     a. Compute shardKey, setShard, find job, set job.setStatus(newStatus), save
     b. Evict and rewrite cache: redisTemplate.opsForValue().set(JobConstants.CACHE_STATUS_PREFIX + id, newStatus, 5, TimeUnit.MINUTES)
     c. clear shard in finally

3. Update workflow-controller/src/main/java/com/orchestrator/controller/service/JobServicePort.java:
   - Add method signature: void updateJobStatus(UUID id, String newStatus)

4. Update workflow-controller/src/main/java/com/orchestrator/controller/web/JobController.java:
   - Add endpoint: PATCH /api/v1/jobs/{id}/status
     - Accepts @RequestBody a simple record or Map<String, String> with a 'status' field
     - Calls jobServicePort.updateJobStatus(id, status)
     - Returns 204 No Content

5. Verify workflow-controller/src/main/resources/application.yml already has:
   spring.redis.host: redis-svc
   spring.redis.port: 6379
   spring.data.redis.host and port referencing the above
   If missing, add them.

6. Create docs/phase2-caching.md explaining:
   - What cache-aside pattern is (check cache first, populate on miss)
   - Why TTL of 5 minutes (balance between freshness and load reduction)
   - Why we cache only status string not the full object (minimize Redis memory)
   - How to verify: kubectl exec into redis pod, run redis-cli keys '*' to see cached entries
   - What happens when Redis is down (job still works via PostgreSQL fallback -- note this in the doc)

Do not run any commands. Only create or modify files.

subtasks.title: Kafka Integration
subtasks.prompt:
You are working inside the folder: c:\Users\piyushvi\Downloads\project\local-ai-orchestrator\orchestrator-parent\ Task: Implement Kafka integration — a producer in workflow-controller that publishes a job event on every submission, and a consumer in job-worker that processes the event and reports completion. 1. Create workflow-controller/src/main/java/com/orchestrator/controller/config/KafkaProducerConfig.java: - @Configuration class - Define ProducerFactory @Bean: - Map of producer configs: BOOTSTRAP_SERVERS_CONFIG from env var KAFKA_BOOTSTRAP_SERVERS (default: kafka-svc:9092) - KEY_SERIALIZER_CLASS_CONFIG: StringSerializer - VALUE_SERIALIZER_CLASS_CONFIG: StringSerializer - Define KafkaTemplate @Bean using the ProducerFactory 2. Create workflow-controller/src/main/java/com/orchestrator/controller/messaging/JobEventPublisher.java: - @Component class - Inject KafkaTemplate - Method: void publishJobSubmitted(UUID jobId, String description, int priority, String shardKey) - Build a JSON string manually: {"jobId":"","description":"","priority":

,"shardKey":""} - Send to topic JobConstants.TOPIC_JOB_SUBMITTED with jobId.toString() as the key - Use kafkaTemplate.send() and add a callback on the ListenableFuture to log success or failure - Wrap in try/catch — Kafka failure must NOT fail the HTTP request (fire-and-forget) 3. Update workflow-controller/src/main/java/com/orchestrator/controller/service/JobService.java: - Inject JobEventPublisher - In submitJob(): after cachePutStatus(), call jobEventPublisher.publishJobSubmitted(job.getId(), job.getDescription(), job.getPriority(), shardKey == 0 ? JobConstants.SHARD_A : JobConstants.SHARD_B) 4. Create job-worker/src/main/java/com/orchestrator/worker/config/KafkaConsumerConfig.java: - @Configuration @EnableKafka class - Define ConsumerFactory @Bean: - BOOTSTRAP_SERVERS_CONFIG from env var KAFKA_BOOTSTRAP_SERVERS (default: kafka-svc:9092) - GROUP_ID_CONFIG: job-worker-group - KEY_DESERIALIZER_CLASS_CONFIG: StringDeserializer - VALUE_DESERIALIZER_CLASS_CONFIG: StringDeserializer - AUTO_OFFSET_RESET_CONFIG: earliest - Define ConcurrentKafkaListenerContainerFactory @Bean using the ConsumerFactory 5. Create job-worker/src/main/java/com/orchestrator/worker/messaging/JobEventConsumer.java: - @Component class - @KafkaListener(topics = "job-submitted", groupId = "job-worker-group", containerFactory = "kafkaListenerContainerFactory") - Method: void handleJobSubmitted(String message) - Parse message manually using simple String operations or Jackson ObjectMapper to extract jobId - Log: "[job-worker] Received job: , processing..." - Simulate work: Thread.sleep(2000) (2 second delay to represent processing) - Call workflow-controller's PATCH endpoint via RestTemplate or WebClient: PATCH http://workflow-controller-svc:8081/api/v1/jobs/{jobId}/status body: {"status":"COMPLETED"} - Log: "[job-worker] Job marked COMPLETED" - Wrap everything in try/catch and log errors without rethrowing (consumer must not crash) 6. Create job-worker/src/main/java/com/orchestrator/worker/config/RestTemplateConfig.java: - @Configuration class - Define RestTemplate @Bean 7. Update job-worker/src/main/resources/application.yml: - Add spring.kafka.bootstrap-servers: ${KAFKA_BOOTSTRAP_SERVERS:kafka-svc:9092} - Add spring.kafka.consumer.group-id: job-worker-group - Add spring.kafka.consumer.auto-offset-reset: earliest - Add workflow.controller.url: ${WORKFLOW_CONTROLLER_URL:http://workflow-controller-svc:8081} 8. Create docs/phase2-kafka.md explaining: - What a Kafka producer does (publishes messages to a topic) - What a Kafka consumer does (subscribes to a topic, processes messages) - Why Kafka decouples the HTTP request from the actual job execution (fire-and-forget) - The full flow: POST /api/v1/jobs -> PostgreSQL + Redis + Kafka -> job-worker picks up -> PATCH status to COMPLETED - What happens if job-worker is down (message waits in Kafka until worker comes back up) - Why consumer group ID matters (only one consumer in the group processes each message) Do not run any commands. Only create or modify files.

subtasks.title: Java 21 Virtual Threads Concurrency
subtasks.prompt:
You are working inside the folder: c:\Users\piyushvi\Downloads\project\local-ai-orchestrator\orchestrator-parent\

Task: Enable Java 21 Virtual Threads in workflow-controller and job-worker so both apps use lightweight virtual threads instead of the fixed Tomcat/OS thread pool for handling concurrent requests and Kafka listener callbacks.

1. Create workflow-controller/src/main/java/com/orchestrator/controller/config/ThreadConfig.java:
   - @Configuration class
   - Define a TomcatProtocolHandlerCustomizer<?> @Bean named virtualThreadTomcatCustomizer:
     - Returns a lambda: protocolHandler -> protocolHandler.setExecutor(Executors.newVirtualThreadPerTaskExecutor())
     - This replaces Tomcat's fixed thread pool with an unbounded virtual thread executor
     - Each incoming HTTP request now runs on its own virtual thread
   - Add a static @Bean of type Executor named virtualThreadExecutor:
     - Returns Executors.newVirtualThreadPerTaskExecutor()
     - This can be injected elsewhere for async tasks
   - Import: java.util.concurrent.Executors, org.apache.catalina.connector.Connector, org.springframework.boot.web.embedded.tomcat.TomcatProtocolHandlerCustomizer

2. Create job-worker/src/main/java/com/orchestrator/worker/config/ThreadConfig.java:
   - @Configuration class
   - Define a ConcurrentKafkaListenerContainerFactory customizer or override the factory from KafkaConsumerConfig:
     - Update the existing kafkaListenerContainerFactory @Bean in KafkaConsumerConfig to set a virtual thread executor:
       factory.getContainerProperties().setListenerTaskExecutor(new SimpleAsyncTaskExecutor(Thread.ofVirtual().factory()))
     - This makes each Kafka listener invocation run on a virtual thread instead of a platform thread
   - Also define a general @Bean Executor named virtualThreadExecutor:
     - Returns Executors.newVirtualThreadPerTaskExecutor()
   - Import: java.util.concurrent.Executors, org.springframework.core.task.SimpleAsyncTaskExecutor

3. Update workflow-controller/src/main/resources/application.yml:
   - Add under spring.threads:
     virtual:
       enabled: true
   - This is the Spring Boot 3.2 property that automatically enables virtual threads for Tomcat, so our manual ThreadConfig acts as a belt-and-suspenders guarantee

4. Update job-worker/src/main/resources/application.yml:
   - Add the same spring.threads.virtual.enabled: true property

5. Update orchestrator-parent/pom.xml:
   - Verify (do not change if already correct) that maven-compiler-plugin is configured with:
     <source>21</source>
     <target>21</target>
     OR <release>21</release>
   - Verify spring-boot-starter-parent or spring-boot-dependencies version is 3.2.x (Virtual Threads support requires Spring Boot 3.2+)
   - If compiler config is missing, add it under <build><plugins>

6. Create docs/phase2-virtual-threads.md explaining:
   - What a platform thread is (1 OS thread, ~1MB stack, limited to ~200-500 per JVM safely)
   - What a virtual thread is (mapped to OS threads by JVM scheduler, ~few KB stack, millions possible)
   - Why this matters for our project: each HTTP request and each Kafka message now gets its own thread without the fixed pool limit
   - The difference between Executors.newVirtualThreadPerTaskExecutor() and a fixed thread pool
   - Why virtual threads are ideal for I/O-bound tasks (PostgreSQL queries, Redis calls, Kafka, HTTP calls) but not for CPU-bound tasks
   - What spring.threads.virtual.enabled: true does in Spring Boot 3.2

Do not run any commands. Only create or modify files.

subtasks.title: Qdrant Client Integration
subtasks.prompt:
You are working inside the folder: c:\Users\piyushvi\Downloads\project\local-ai-orchestrator\orchestrator-parent\

Task: Integrate Qdrant vector database into workflow-controller for semantic job similarity search. This enables the system to find past similar jobs and reuse their routing decisions instead of calling the LLM every time.

1. Update workflow-controller/pom.xml:
   - Add dependency: io.qdrant:qdrant-client:1.9.1
   - Add dependency: com.fasterxml.jackson.core:jackson-databind (already in Spring Boot BOM, just declare it)

2. Create workflow-controller/src/main/java/com/orchestrator/controller/config/QdrantConfig.java:
   - @Configuration class
   - Read env vars: QDRANT_HOST (default: qdrant-svc), QDRANT_PORT (default: 6334)
   - Define QdrantClient @Bean:
     - Use QdrantClient.newBuilder().setHost(host).setPort(port).build()
     - This creates a gRPC connection to Qdrant
   - Define a String @Bean named qdrantCollectionName returning "job-embeddings"

3. Create workflow-controller/src/main/java/com/orchestrator/controller/ai/EmbeddingService.java:
   - @Service class
   - Inject RestTemplate (already defined via a @Bean somewhere, or create one here if not)
   - Read env var OLLAMA_BASE_URL (default: http://ollama-svc:11434)
   - Method: float[] embed(String text)
     a. Call Ollama embed API: POST {ollamaBaseUrl}/api/embed
        Body: {"model":"nomic-embed-text","input":"<text>"}
     b. Parse response JSON: {"embeddings":[[0.1, 0.2, ...]]} -> extract float array from embeddings[0]
     c. Return the float[]
     d. On any error, log warning and return null
   - Use RestTemplate for the HTTP call with a Map<String, Object> request body
   - Parse response with Jackson ObjectMapper

4. Create workflow-controller/src/main/java/com/orchestrator/controller/ai/QdrantService.java:
   - @Service class
   - Inject QdrantClient and String qdrantCollectionName
   - Method: Optional<String> findSimilarRouting(float[] embedding)
     a. Build a SearchPoints request using Qdrant client:
        SearchPoints.newBuilder()
          .setCollectionName(collectionName)
          .addAllVector(floatArrayToList(embedding))
          .setLimit(1)
          .setWithPayload(WithPayloadSelector.newBuilder().setEnable(true).build())
          .build()
     b. Execute: List<ScoredPoint> results = qdrantClient.searchAsync(request).get()
     c. If results is empty or results.get(0).getScore() < 0.9f -> return Optional.empty()
     d. Extract payload field "routingDecision" from results.get(0).getPayload()
     e. Return Optional.of(routingDecision string)
     f. Wrap in try/catch, return Optional.empty() on any error
   - Method: void storeJobEmbedding(UUID jobId, float[] embedding, String routingDecision)
     a. Build PointStruct:
        PointStruct.newBuilder()
          .setId(PointId.newBuilder().setUuid(jobId.toString()).build())
          .setVectors(Vectors.newBuilder().setVector(Vector.newBuilder().addAllData(floatArrayToList(embedding)).build()))
          .putPayload("routingDecision", Value.newBuilder().setStringValue(routingDecision).build())
          .putPayload("jobId", Value.newBuilder().setStringValue(jobId.toString()).build())
          .build()
     b. Upsert: qdrantClient.upsertAsync(collectionName, List.of(point)).get()
     c. Wrap in try/catch and log errors without rethrowing
   - Private helper: List<Float> floatArrayToList(float[] arr)

5. Update workflow-controller/src/main/resources/application.yml:
   - Add:
     qdrant:
       host: ${QDRANT_HOST:qdrant-svc}
       port: ${QDRANT_PORT:6334}
     ollama:
       base-url: ${OLLAMA_BASE_URL:http://ollama-svc:11434}

6. Create docs/phase3-qdrant-integration.md explaining:
   - What vector similarity search is: find the most similar past job by comparing float vectors
   - Why score threshold 0.9: below 0.9 means not similar enough, call LLM instead
   - What nomic-embed-text model does: converts text to 384-dim float vector
   - The difference between gRPC port 6334 and REST port 6333
   - How the cache saves LLM calls: first time a job type is seen -> LLM called -> stored in Qdrant; next similar job -> Qdrant hit -> LLM skipped

Do not run any commands. Only create or modify files.

subtasks.title: Ollama LLM Routing Service
subtasks.prompt:
You are working inside the folder: c:\Users\piyushvi\Downloads\project\local-ai-orchestrator\orchestrator-parent\

Task: Implement the Ollama LLM routing service in workflow-controller. This service calls the local Mistral 7B model to analyze a job description and return a structured routing decision (which worker type should handle it, estimated duration, suggested priority).

1. Create workflow-controller/src/main/java/com/orchestrator/controller/ai/OllamaService.java:
   - @Service class
   - Inject RestTemplate and ObjectMapper
   - Read @Value("${ollama.base-url:http://ollama-svc:11434}") String ollamaBaseUrl
   - Method: RoutingDecision route(String jobDescription)
     a. Build the prompt string:
        "You are a job routing system. Analyze this job and respond ONLY with valid JSON, no explanation, no markdown.
        Job: <jobDescription>
        Respond with exactly this JSON structure:
        {\"workerType\": \"<one of: data-processing, notification, deployment, analysis, general>\",
        \"estimatedSeconds\": <integer between 1 and 300>,
        \"suggestedPriority\": <integer between 1 and 10>,
        \"reasoning\": \"<one sentence max>\"}"
     b. Build request body: Map with keys \"model\"->\"mistral:7b\", \"prompt\"->promptString, \"stream\"->false
     c. POST to ollamaBaseUrl + \"/api/generate\" using RestTemplate
     d. Parse response JSON: extract the \"response\" field (this is the model's text output)
     e. Extract the JSON object from within the response string using a simple regex or by finding first { and last }
     f. Parse extracted JSON into RoutingDecision record
     g. On any error (timeout, parse failure, model not loaded): log warning and return RoutingDecision.defaultDecision()
     h. Set a 30 second read timeout on RestTemplate for this call
   - The RestTemplate used here should have a 30s timeout -- inject it with @Qualifier("ollamaRestTemplate")

2. Create workflow-controller/src/main/java/com/orchestrator/controller/ai/RoutingDecision.java:
   - A Java record with fields: String workerType, int estimatedSeconds, int suggestedPriority, String reasoning
   - Add a static factory method: RoutingDecision defaultDecision() that returns RoutingDecision(\"general\", 30, 5, \"default routing\")
   - Add a method: String toJson() that manually builds a JSON string from the fields

3. Create workflow-controller/src/main/java/com/orchestrator/controller/config/OllamaRestTemplateConfig.java:
   - @Configuration class
   - Define a RestTemplate @Bean named \"ollamaRestTemplate\":
     - Use RestTemplateBuilder or SimpleClientHttpRequestFactory
     - Set connectTimeout: 5 seconds
     - Set readTimeout: 30 seconds (Mistral 7B takes 2-5s on CPU, give generous margin)
   - Keep the existing default RestTemplate @Bean (no timeout) for other calls (Redis, Qdrant)

4. Update workflow-controller/src/main/java/com/orchestrator/controller/model/Job.java:
   - Add field: String workerType (nullable, default null)
   - Add field: String routingDecision (nullable -- stores full JSON of RoutingDecision)
   - Add @Column(name = \"worker_type\") and @Column(name = \"routing_decision\") annotations

5. Update k8s/infra/postgres/init-jobs-table.sql:
   - Add two new columns to the jobs table:
     ALTER TABLE jobs ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS worker_type VARCHAR(50);
     ALTER TABLE jobs ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS routing_decision TEXT;

6. Update workflow-controller/src/main/java/com/orchestrator/controller/service/JobService.java:
   - Inject OllamaService
   - In submitJob(): after building the Job entity but before saving:
     a. Call OllamaService.route(request.description()) -> RoutingDecision decision
     b. Set job.setWorkerType(decision.workerType())
     c. Set job.setRoutingDecision(decision.toJson())
     d. Set job.setPriority(decision.suggestedPriority()) if request did not explicitly provide priority (i.e. if request.priority() == 5 which is default)
   - Keep all existing sharding and caching logic unchanged

7. Update orchestrator-parent/common/src/main/java/com/orchestrator/common/dto/JobResponse.java:
   - Add field: String workerType to the record
   - Update all places in JobService that construct JobResponse to include workerType

8. Create docs/phase3-ollama-routing.md explaining:
   - What the prompt does: instructs Mistral to respond ONLY with JSON (avoids explanation text)
   - Why stream:false: we want complete response at once, not token by token
   - Why 30s timeout: CPU inference is slow, 5s average but can spike
   - The 5 worker types and what kind of jobs map to each
   - What happens on LLM failure: defaultDecision() ensures the job still gets submitted

Do not run any commands. Only create or modify files.

subtasks.title: Dockerfiles for Spring Boot Modules
subtasks.prompt:
You are working inside the folder: c:\Users\piyushvi\Downloads\project\local-ai-orchestrator\orchestrator-parent\

Task: Create multi-stage Dockerfiles for api-gateway, workflow-controller, and job-worker. Each Dockerfile builds the JAR using Maven then packages it into a lean runtime image.

1. Create orchestrator-parent/api-gateway/Dockerfile:
   - Stage 1 (builder): FROM eclipse-temurin:21-jdk-alpine AS builder
     - WORKDIR /build
     - Copy parent pom.xml: COPY pom.xml ./
     - Copy common module: COPY common/ ./common/
     - Copy api-gateway module: COPY api-gateway/ ./api-gateway/
     - Run Maven build: RUN ./mvnw -s .mvn/settings.xml -pl api-gateway -am clean package -DskipTests
     - NOTE: mvnw may not exist -- use: RUN apk add --no-cache maven && mvn -s .mvn/settings.xml -pl api-gateway -am clean package -DskipTests
   - Stage 2 (runtime): FROM eclipse-temurin:21-jre-alpine
     - WORKDIR /app
     - COPY --from=builder /build/api-gateway/target/api-gateway-*.jar app.jar
     - EXPOSE 8080
     - ENTRYPOINT ["java", "-jar", "app.jar"]

2. Create orchestrator-parent/workflow-controller/Dockerfile:
   - Same multi-stage pattern as api-gateway
   - Stage 1: copy pom.xml, common/, workflow-controller/, build with -pl workflow-controller -am
   - Stage 2: copy workflow-controller/target/workflow-controller-*.jar, EXPOSE 8081
   - Add JVM tuning flags to ENTRYPOINT for container awareness:
     ENTRYPOINT ["java", "-XX:+UseContainerSupport", "-XX:MaxRAMPercentage=75.0", "-jar", "app.jar"]

3. Create orchestrator-parent/job-worker/Dockerfile:
   - Same multi-stage pattern
   - Stage 1: copy pom.xml, common/, job-worker/, build with -pl job-worker -am
   - Stage 2: copy job-worker/target/job-worker-*.jar, EXPOSE 8082
   - Same JVM tuning flags as workflow-controller

4. Create orchestrator-parent/.dockerignore:
   - Add: target/, .git/, .mvn/repository, *.md, node_modules/
   - This prevents Maven target directories from being copied into Docker build context

5. Create a helper script scripts/build-images.ps1:
   - Set DOCKER_BUILDKIT=1 for faster builds
   - Build each image from the orchestrator-parent directory (important -- Dockerfile COPY paths are relative to orchestrator-parent):
     docker build -f api-gateway/Dockerfile -t local-ai/api-gateway:latest .
     docker build -f workflow-controller/Dockerfile -t local-ai/workflow-controller:latest .
     docker build -f job-worker/Dockerfile -t local-ai/job-worker:latest .
   - After each build, load into Minikube: minikube image load local-ai/<name>:latest
   - Print image sizes after loading: docker images local-ai/*
   - Use plain ASCII characters only, no unicode

6. Create docs/phase4-dockerfiles.md explaining:
   - What a multi-stage build is and why it matters (builder has JDK + Maven ~500MB, runtime only has JRE ~100MB)
   - Why eclipse-temurin:21-jre-alpine (smallest JRE image, Alpine Linux = 5MB base)
   - What XX:UseContainerSupport does (JVM reads cgroup limits instead of host RAM)
   - What XX:MaxRAMPercentage=75.0 does (JVM heap = 75% of container memory limit)
   - Why we build from orchestrator-parent directory (Maven multi-module needs parent pom.xml in context)

Do not run any commands. Only create the files.

subtasks.title: Kubernetes Manifests for App Pods
subtasks.prompt:
You are working inside the folder: c:\Users\piyushvi\Downloads\project\local-ai-orchestrator\

Task: Create Kubernetes manifests for the three Spring Boot application pods so they can run inside the Minikube cluster alongside the infrastructure pods deployed in Phase 1.

Context:
- All images are named local-ai/api-gateway:latest, local-ai/workflow-controller:latest, local-ai/job-worker:latest
- Images are already loaded into Minikube via 'minikube image load' (NOT pulled from Docker Hub)
- Namespace: ai-orchestrator
- Infra service names already running: postgres-shard-a-svc:5432, postgres-shard-b-svc:5432, redis-svc:6379, kafka-svc:9092, qdrant-svc:6333, ollama-svc:11434

1. Create k8s/app/api-gateway.yaml:
   - ConfigMap named api-gateway-config with: SPRING_PROFILES_ACTIVE=k8s
   - Deployment named api-gateway with 1 replica:
     - image: local-ai/api-gateway:latest
     - imagePullPolicy: Never (image is already in Minikube, do NOT pull from registry)
     - port: 8080
     - envFrom: configMapRef api-gateway-config
     - resources: requests 256Mi/250m, limits 512Mi/500m
     - readinessProbe: httpGet /actuator/health port 8080, initialDelaySeconds 30, periodSeconds 10, failureThreshold 5
     - livenessProbe: httpGet /actuator/health port 8080, initialDelaySeconds 60, periodSeconds 20, failureThreshold 3
   - Service named api-gateway-svc:
     - type: NodePort (so we can reach it from Windows host via minikube service or minikube ip)
     - port 8080 -> targetPort 8080 -> nodePort 30080

2. Create k8s/app/workflow-controller.yaml:
   - ConfigMap named workflow-controller-config with:
     DB_SHARD_A_URL=jdbc:postgresql://postgres-shard-a-svc:5432/orchestrator
     DB_SHARD_B_URL=jdbc:postgresql://postgres-shard-b-svc:5432/orchestrator
     DB_USER=admin
     DB_PASSWORD=admin123
     KAFKA_BOOTSTRAP_SERVERS=kafka-svc:9092
     QDRANT_HOST=qdrant-svc
     QDRANT_PORT=6333
     OLLAMA_BASE_URL=http://ollama-svc:11434
     SPRING_REDIS_HOST=redis-svc
     SPRING_REDIS_PORT=6379
   - Deployment named workflow-controller with 1 replica:
     - image: local-ai/workflow-controller:latest
     - imagePullPolicy: Never
     - port: 8081
     - envFrom: configMapRef workflow-controller-config
     - resources: requests 512Mi/500m, limits 1Gi/1000m
     - readinessProbe: httpGet /actuator/health port 8081, initialDelaySeconds 45, periodSeconds 10, failureThreshold 6
     - livenessProbe: httpGet /actuator/health port 8081, initialDelaySeconds 90, periodSeconds 20, failureThreshold 3
   - Service named workflow-controller-svc:
     - type: ClusterIP
     - port 8081 -> targetPort 8081

3. Create k8s/app/job-worker.yaml:
   - ConfigMap named job-worker-config with:
     KAFKA_BOOTSTRAP_SERVERS=kafka-svc:9092
     WORKFLOW_CONTROLLER_URL=http://workflow-controller-svc:8081
     OLLAMA_BASE_URL=http://ollama-svc:11434
     QDRANT_HOST=qdrant-svc
     QDRANT_PORT=6333
   - Deployment named job-worker with 1 replica:
     - image: local-ai/job-worker:latest
     - imagePullPolicy: Never
     - port: 8082
     - envFrom: configMapRef job-worker-config
     - resources: requests 512Mi/500m, limits 1Gi/1000m
     - readinessProbe: httpGet /actuator/health port 8082, initialDelaySeconds 45, periodSeconds 10, failureThreshold 6
     - livenessProbe: httpGet /actuator/health port 8082, initialDelaySeconds 90, periodSeconds 20, failureThreshold 3
   - Service named job-worker-svc:
     - type: ClusterIP
     - port 8082 -> targetPort 8082

4. Create scripts/deploy-apps.ps1:
   - $ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'
   - Preflight: check kubectl and minikube are available, check minikube is Running, check namespace ai-orchestrator exists
   - Apply all three manifests from the project root:
     kubectl apply -f k8s/app/workflow-controller.yaml
     kubectl apply -f k8s/app/job-worker.yaml
     kubectl apply -f k8s/app/api-gateway.yaml
   - Wait for each deployment to be ready:
     kubectl rollout status deployment/workflow-controller -n ai-orchestrator --timeout=180s
     kubectl rollout status deployment/job-worker -n ai-orchestrator --timeout=180s
     kubectl rollout status deployment/api-gateway -n ai-orchestrator --timeout=120s
   - Print pod status: kubectl get pods -n ai-orchestrator
   - Print the NodePort URL: minikube service api-gateway-svc -n ai-orchestrator --url
   - Use colored Write-Host output (Green for success, Red for errors, Cyan for steps)
   - NOTE: deploy workflow-controller BEFORE job-worker (job-worker calls workflow-controller on startup via Kafka)

5. Update scripts/deploy-infra.ps1:
   - At the very bottom, after the final 'All infrastructure deployed and Ready.' message, add a note:
     Write-Host 'Next: run scripts/deploy-apps.ps1 to deploy Spring Boot application pods.' -ForegroundColor Yellow

6. Update scripts/safe-shutdown.ps1:
   - Before shutting down infra, also scale down app deployments to 0 if they exist:
     kubectl scale deployment api-gateway workflow-controller job-worker --replicas=0 -n ai-orchestrator 2>$null
   - Add this BEFORE any kubectl delete commands, and wrap in try/catch so missing deployments don't fail the script

7. Create docs/phase4-k8s-deploy.md explaining:
   - Why imagePullPolicy: Never (images are already loaded into Minikube, pulling from registry would fail for local-ai/* images)
   - What a ConfigMap is and why we use it instead of hardcoding env vars in the Deployment spec
   - Why workflow-controller gets higher memory limits than api-gateway (it connects to DB, Redis, Kafka, does sharding logic)
   - Why job-worker has no NodePort (it is a pure Kafka consumer, no inbound HTTP from outside)
   - Why we deploy workflow-controller before job-worker (Kafka consumer starts immediately and calls workflow-controller for status updates)
   - How to test end-to-end: minikube service api-gateway-svc -n ai-orchestrator --url, then curl POST /api/v1/jobs
   - How to view logs: kubectl logs deployment/workflow-controller -n ai-orchestrator -f

Do not run any commands. Only create or modify files.

subtasks.title: End-to-End AI Routing Flow
subtasks.prompt:
You are working inside the folder: c:\Users\piyushvi\Downloads\project\local-ai-orchestrator\orchestrator-parent\

Task: Wire the complete async AI routing pipeline. The HTTP request must return instantly. The job-worker reads from Kafka, embeds the description, checks Qdrant cache, calls Ollama only on cache miss, stores embedding in Qdrant, then updates the job status and workerType via HTTP back to workflow-controller.

IMPORTANT DESIGN: Option B -- Ollama and Qdrant are called by job-worker AFTER Kafka delivery, NOT during the HTTP request.

--- PART 1: workflow-controller changes ---

1. Update workflow-controller/src/main/java/com/orchestrator/controller/service/JobService.java:
   - In submitJob(): REMOVE the OllamaService.route() call and all workerType/routingDecision enrichment
   - submitJob() should only: generate UUID, compute shardKey, build Job (workerType=null, routingDecision=null), save to PostgreSQL, cache status in Redis, publish to Kafka, return JobResponse
   - Keep all sharding, Redis caching, and Kafka publishing logic unchanged
   - REMOVE OllamaService injection from constructor (it is no longer needed here)

2. Add a new endpoint to workflow-controller for the job-worker to report routing results:
   - Add to workflow-controller/src/main/java/com/orchestrator/controller/service/JobServicePort.java:
     void updateJobRouting(UUID id, String workerType, String routingDecision)
   - Add to workflow-controller/src/main/java/com/orchestrator/controller/service/JobService.java:
     public void updateJobRouting(UUID id, String workerType, String routingDecision)
     a. Compute shardKey, setShard, findById (throw NOT_FOUND if missing)
     b. job.setWorkerType(workerType)
     c. job.setRoutingDecision(routingDecision)
     d. job.setStatus("ROUTED")
     e. jobRepository.save(job)
     f. cachePutStatus(id, "ROUTED")
     g. ShardContextHolder.clear() in finally
   - Add to workflow-controller/src/main/java/com/orchestrator/controller/web/JobController.java:
     PATCH /api/v1/jobs/{id}/routing
     Accepts @RequestBody JobRoutingUpdateRequest (a new record with fields: String workerType, String routingDecision)
     Calls jobServicePort.updateJobRouting(id, body.workerType(), body.routingDecision())
     Returns 204 No Content
   - Create workflow-controller/src/main/java/com/orchestrator/controller/web/JobRoutingUpdateRequest.java:
     public record JobRoutingUpdateRequest(@NotBlank String workerType, String routingDecision) {}

3. Add "ROUTED" as a valid status between PENDING and COMPLETED so dashboard can show routing progress.
   No code change needed -- it is just a string value. Document it in the existing JobConstants.java by adding:
   public static final String STATUS_ROUTED = "ROUTED";

--- PART 2: job-worker changes ---

4. Add dependencies to job-worker/pom.xml:
   - spring-boot-starter-web (for RestTemplate and HTTP client use)
   - spring-boot-starter-data-redis (to share EmbeddingService/QdrantService via HTTP -- actually just needs RestTemplate)
   - Actually only add: spring-boot-starter-web if not already present

5. Copy/create the AI service classes in job-worker (they cannot import from workflow-controller -- different module):
   - Create job-worker/src/main/java/com/orchestrator/worker/ai/EmbeddingService.java:
     Exact copy of workflow-controller EmbeddingService -- same logic, same RestTemplate injection, same Ollama embed API call
   - Create job-worker/src/main/java/com/orchestrator/worker/ai/OllamaService.java:
     Exact copy of workflow-controller OllamaService and RoutingDecision -- same logic
   - Create job-worker/src/main/java/com/orchestrator/worker/ai/RoutingDecision.java:
     Exact copy of workflow-controller RoutingDecision record
   - Create job-worker/src/main/java/com/orchestrator/worker/ai/QdrantService.java:
     Exact copy of workflow-controller QdrantService -- same REST API calls

6. Create job-worker/src/main/java/com/orchestrator/worker/config/AiConfig.java:
   - @Configuration class
   - @Bean(name = "ollamaRestTemplate"): SimpleClientHttpRequestFactory with connectTimeout=5000, readTimeout=30000
   - @Bean(name = "qdrantBaseUrl"): reads QDRANT_HOST (default qdrant-svc) and QDRANT_PORT (default 6333), returns "http://host:port"
   - @Bean(name = "qdrantCollectionName"): returns "job-embeddings"
   - @Bean ObjectMapper: returns new ObjectMapper()

7. Update job-worker/src/main/java/com/orchestrator/worker/messaging/JobEventConsumer.java:
   Replace the current simple consumer (Thread.sleep + PATCH status COMPLETED) with the full AI routing pipeline:
   - Inject EmbeddingService, QdrantService, OllamaService, RestTemplate (default), @Value("${workflow.controller.url}") String controllerUrl
   - In handleJobSubmitted(String message):
     a. Parse jobId AND description from Kafka message JSON
     b. Step 1 -- Embed: float[] vector = embeddingService.embed(description)
     c. Step 2 -- Check Qdrant: Optional<String> cached = qdrantService.findSimilarRouting(vector)
     d. Step 3a -- Cache HIT: workerType = cached.get(), routingDecision = "{\"source\":\"qdrant-cache\",\"workerType\":\"" + workerType + "\"}"
     e. Step 3b -- Cache MISS: RoutingDecision decision = ollamaService.route(description); workerType = decision.workerType(); routingDecision = decision.toJson(); then call qdrantService.storeJobEmbedding(jobId, vector, workerType)
     f. Step 4 -- PATCH routing to workflow-controller: PATCH {controllerUrl}/api/v1/jobs/{jobId}/routing body: {"workerType":"<wt>","routingDecision":"<rd>"}
     g. Step 5 -- Simulate execution: Thread.sleep(2000)
     h. Step 6 -- PATCH status COMPLETED: PATCH {controllerUrl}/api/v1/jobs/{jobId}/status body: {"status":"COMPLETED"}
     i. Log each step with jobId
     j. Wrap entire method in try/catch -- on any failure log and skip to COMPLETED to avoid stuck jobs

   - Add ollama.base-url: ${OLLAMA_BASE_URL:http://ollama-svc:11434}
   - Add qdrant.host: ${QDRANT_HOST:qdrant-svc}
   - Add qdrant.port: ${QDRANT_PORT:6333}

   - The Qdrant cache hit/miss flow and why it saves LLM calls
